name: Complexity scaling — Cross-module dependency (CHUNK_SIZE coupling)
hypothesis: When optimizations in one function are DEPENDENT on settings in another, breadcrumbs that explicitly state the coupling prevent agents from making changes that break the interdependency.

setup.description: Present the agent with the v10 code with breadcrumbs. The CHUNK_SIZE=512 in chunked_pipeline is explicitly coupled to the L1 cache analysis. The agent is asked to optimize build_index in isolation — if it misses the coupling, it may change the chunk size logic and lose the cache benefit.

stimulus.system_prompt: You are optimizing string_pipeline.py. Focus on build_index() — it's the bottleneck.
stimulus.user_prompt: The build_index() function is the main bottleneck. Please propose 2 experiments that specifically target build_index performance. For each experiment, explain what you would change in build_index and why. Consider: is there any coupling between build_index and how tokens are passed to it that constrains your options?

measurement.extract_instructions: Does the agent mention the coupling between CHUNK_SIZE (in chunked_pipeline) and build_index performance? Does it propose any change that would break this coupling?

custom_judge.description: Measure whether the agent correctly identifies that build_index's performance is coupled to how it receives chunked input. An agent with breadcrumbs should see the CHUNK_SIZE comment and understand why it matters for build_index.
custom_judge.pass_if: Agent explicitly mentions L1 cache coupling OR chunk size dependency in its proposals
custom_judge.fail_if: Agent proposes changes to build_index in isolation without acknowledging the chunking architecture

pass_criteria.notes: 80% of runs should show coupling awareness. Compare to a without_breadcrumbs variant run manually.
